title: Catalyst Center Data Collection Health (Meta)
dataSources: `index=catalyst sourcetype=cisco:dnac:*` (all Catalyst Center sourcetypes) plus `index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd component=ExecProcessor "TA_cisco_catalyst"` (TA execution health). This meta use case monitors the health of Catalyst Center data collection itself — checking for event gaps, stale data, TA errors, and API failures across all 15+ sourcetype families.
spl: index=catalyst sourcetype="cisco:dnac:*" | stats count as event_count latest(_time) as last_event earliest(_time) as first_event by sourcetype | eval hours_since_last=round((now()-last_event)/3600,1) | eval status=if(hours_since_last>2,"Stale","Active") | sort -hours_since_last
description: Monitors the health of the Catalyst Center data collection pipeline by checking event volume and freshness for each sourcetype, detecting collection failures or gaps.
value: All other Catalyst Center use cases depend on data flowing reliably. This meta-monitoring UC ensures the pipeline is healthy and catches collection failures early.
implementation:
This UC uses existing TA inputs — no additional configuration needed beyond the standard TA setup. It monitors all `cisco:dnac:*` sourcetypes to verify:

1. **Event volume:** Each sourcetype should have events within the last 2 hours (based on TA polling intervals)
2. **Expected sourcetypes:** devicehealth, clienthealth, networkhealth, issue, compliance, securityadvisory, client, audit:logs, site:topology
3. **Freshness:** If any sourcetype's latest event is older than 2 hours, the TA input may have failed

Schedule this search as a daily health check and alert when any sourcetype shows 'Stale' status.
visualization: Table: sourcetype, event_count, hours_since_last, status; heatmap for Stale vs Active; optional trend of event_count per sourcetype over 7 days.
knownFalsePositives:
**Low-frequency inputs appearing stale when checked against a tight freshness threshold.** Some Catalyst Center API endpoints (site hierarchy, SWIM golden image, license) are polled hourly or less frequently. If the data freshness check uses a 15-minute threshold, these inputs will always appear stale. Distinguish by checking the configured poll interval for each input type in the TA configuration. Suppress by maintaining a `catalyst_input_intervals` lookup with expected poll intervals per sourcetype and comparing freshness against the per-sourcetype threshold.

**TA poll failure due to Catalyst Center API rate limiting.** If the TA exceeds Catalyst Center's API rate limits, some inputs may fail to collect data, appearing as stale. Distinguish by checking `index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd component=ExecProcessor "TA_cisco_catalyst"` for rate limit errors (HTTP 429). Suppress by adjusting the TA poll intervals to stay within the rate limit.

**Catalyst Center platform maintenance causing a temporary collection gap.** During Catalyst Center upgrades or restarts, the API is unavailable and the TA cannot collect data. Distinguish by checking whether the collection gap coincides with a Catalyst Center maintenance window. Suppress by annotating data freshness gaps with maintenance window records.

**Splunk forwarder or heavy forwarder restart causing a brief collection gap.** When the Splunk instance running the TA restarts, there is a brief gap in data collection. Distinguish by checking `index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd "Splunkd starting" OR "Splunkd restarting"` for restart events coinciding with the collection gap. Suppress by allowing a 10-minute gap after a forwarder restart before flagging data freshness issues.

references.title: Catalyst Center Intent API Reference — Cisco DevNet

detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Cisco Catalyst Add-on for Splunk (Splunkbase 7538) installed with at least one modular input enabled to `index=catalyst`.
• At least 7 days of data across all enabled sourcetypes to establish baseline freshness expectations.
• Access to `index=_internal` for TA health monitoring.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
This UC is a meta-monitoring use case that validates the health of all Catalyst Center data collection pipelines. It does not collect additional data — it monitors the freshness and completeness of data already being collected.

Expected sourcetypes and their poll intervals:
• `cisco:dnac:devicehealth` — 900s (15 min)
• `cisco:dnac:clienthealth` — 900s (15 min)
• `cisco:dnac:networkhealth` — 900s (15 min)
• `cisco:dnac:issue` — 900s (15 min)
• `cisco:dnac:compliance` — 3600s (1 hour)
• `cisco:dnac:securityadvisory` — 3600s (1 hour)
• `cisco:dnac:device` — 3600s (1 hour)
• `cisco:dnac:site` — 3600s (1 hour)
• `cisco:dnac:swim` — 3600s (1 hour)
• `cisco:dnac:audit:logs` — 300-900s
• `cisco:dnac:event:notification` — real-time (webhook)
• `cisco:dnac:license` — 3600s (1 hour)

Step 2 — Create the search and alert

```spl
index=catalyst sourcetype="cisco:dnac:*"
| stats count as event_count latest(_time) as last_event earliest(_time) as first_event by sourcetype
| eval hours_since_last=round((now()-last_event)/3600,1)
| eval expected_interval_hrs=case(match(sourcetype,"devicehealth|clienthealth|networkhealth|issue"), 0.25, match(sourcetype,"audit"), 0.25, match(sourcetype,"event:notification"), 0.08, 1==1, 1.0)
| eval status=if(hours_since_last > expected_interval_hrs * 3, "Stale", "Active")
| eval days_of_data=round((last_event-first_event)/86400,1)
| sort -hours_since_last
| table sourcetype, event_count, hours_since_last, expected_interval_hrs, status, days_of_data
```

Understanding this SPL:
• **`sourcetype="cisco:dnac:*"`**: Matches all Catalyst Center sourcetypes, providing a comprehensive collection health overview.
• **`expected_interval_hrs`**: Sets the expected freshness threshold per sourcetype family. Fast-polling inputs (devicehealth, issue) expect data every 15 minutes (0.25 hrs). Slow-polling inputs (compliance, SWIM) expect hourly data.
• **`status=if(hours_since_last > expected_interval_hrs * 3, ...)`**: Flags a sourcetype as "Stale" if the last event is more than 3x the expected interval. The 3x multiplier accounts for occasional poll delays without over-alerting.
• **`days_of_data`**: Shows the data retention depth — useful for verifying that sufficient history exists for trend analysis.

Step 3 — Validate
• **Coverage check:** Compare the sourcetype list in Splunk with the enabled inputs in the TA. Every enabled input should have a corresponding sourcetype with "Active" status.
• **Freshness check:** After a known event (e.g., creating a device health poll), verify the corresponding sourcetype shows a recent `last_event` within the expected interval.
• **TA health cross-reference:** `index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd component=ExecProcessor "TA_cisco_catalyst"` should show successful execution for each input.

Step 4 — Operationalize
• **Dashboard:** Traffic-light table showing each sourcetype with green (Active) or red (Stale) status. Add a timechart showing event volume per sourcetype over 24 hours to identify collection patterns.
• **Alert:** Schedule every 30 minutes; trigger when any sourcetype shows "Stale" status. Route to the Splunk admin team. Include the `sourcetype`, `hours_since_last`, and `expected_interval_hrs` in the alert.
• **Dependency for other UCs:** If this UC shows stale data, all downstream Catalyst Center UCs are affected. Prioritize collection health fixes.

Step 5 — Troubleshoot
• **Single sourcetype stale:** The specific modular input may have failed. Check `index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd component=ExecProcessor "TA_cisco_catalyst"` for errors related to that input. Restart the input.
• **All sourcetypes stale:** The TA service account's API token may have expired, or the heavy forwarder is offline. Check API credentials and forwarder connectivity.
• **Event notifications (webhook) stale:** The HEC endpoint or Catalyst Center webhook destination may have failed. See UC-5.13.66 for webhook delivery troubleshooting.
• **Low-frequency inputs appearing stale:** Site hierarchy and license inputs poll hourly. If the freshness threshold is too tight, increase the multiplier or use a per-sourcetype `catalyst_input_intervals` lookup.
• **No data for some sourcetypes:** verify each modular input is enabled in the TA; check `index=_internal` for API error or endpoint timeout messages per input.
• **NULL or missing values across multiple inputs:** a credential or connectivity issue affects all inputs; check the base URL and authentication token first.
• **Fewer events than expected:** check the service account role permissions; credential access denied errors indicate the account lacks required RBAC roles.
grandmaExplanation: We watch the data pipeline itself to make sure all the Catalyst Center information is flowing into Splunk properly, catching any problems with the data feed.
